purpose: M16.3 cheapest-first: re-measure the existing bit-exact staged-rotate fusion flags on the current tree (post M15.x/M16.4) to see if the kernel-time trade shifted enough to promote them. It did not.
workload: quicksort prompt, decode-tokens=32, candidate-budget=3, chain-attn-mode=batched, proposal_impl=persistent_device

baseline_default.note: current default (selected_moe_down_staged on, input rotates unfused)

conclusion: Both staged-rotate flags stay default-OFF on the current tree. Removing ~68-146 small-grid rotate launches via HBM-staged-keyed-barrier kernels REGRESSES C_B (4.67 -> 5.06-5.13) even though it is bit-exact. The ~5.6 us/launch dispatch floor saved (M16.1) is smaller than the barrier-spin + staged HBM round-trip added. Empirical proof on the current tree that op-pair staging fusion cannot reach the C_B<=2 goal -- consistent with M13.B.1/M15.4.
implication_for_m16.3: The first true megakernel must consolidate REAL work + HBM intermediate traffic across big-grid GEMVs (e.g. fuse the selected-expert FFN gate_up->silu->down->combine so the 512-d intermediate stays on-chip and the gate_up-output write + down-input read vanish, collapsing ~3 big-grid launches/layer into 1). Removing small-grid plumbing launches (rotate/rmsnorm/router) is at best neutral and at worst negative.
